title: quercus robur var. brachyphylla
intro: Quercus robur var. brachyphylla, also known as the pedunculate oak dwarf, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the pedunculate oak tree. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its high content of tannins and flavonoids. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as diarrhea, fever, and wounds, and is also believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against infections and promote overall well-being. Quercus robur var. brachyphylla can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and provide additional health benefits.
